title: Malty and Intense Croatian Petit Pois Paella
description: This traditional Croatian dish is a rich, malty paella that features the fresh taste of petit pois and a pescatarian-friendly score-fat sauce.

ingredients.name: score-fat (smoked bacon fat)
ingredients.notes: reserve the drippings from cooking the bacon

instructions: Heat the score-fat in a molcajete over medium heat, then add 1 small onion, diced, and cook until softened.
Add 2 cloves of garlic, minced, and cook for 1 minute.
Add 1 cup of petit pois, rinsed and drained, and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until tender.
Add 1 cup of uncooked Spanish rice, and stir to coat the rice in the score-fat and mix with the petit pois.
Add 1 cup of fish stock, heated, and bring to a simmer.
Add 1 teaspoon of paprika, and 1 teaspoon of saffron, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Fluff the paella with a fork, and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and lemon wedges, if desired.
